title: Leionema gracile
intro: Leionema gracile, also known as the mountain pepper, is a small, woody shrub native to eastern Australia. The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in relieving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Leionema gracile include essential oils, particularly a compound called piperonal, which has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
taxonomy: Leionema gracile is classified within the family Rutaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as citrus or rue family. It belongs to the genus Leionema, which is a group of plants that are native to Australia. Leionema gracile is further classified as a species within the genus, and it is recognized by its unique characteristics and traits. The classification of Leionema gracile is based on its morphology, anatomy, and molecular characteristics. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and the position of Leionema gracile within the plant kingdom.

parts: Leionema gracile, also known as the Graceful Leionema, is a plant species native to Australia.
